prs.title: feat: add engagement optimization assistant with agent state, audit trail, and reporting.
fix: support diff markers in plaintext & markdown code blocks fixes #962

labels.name: seems-ai-generated
labels.description: We label this as a rejection to PR that seems to be AI generated. Please do not do that.

prs.body: This PR adds built-in support for [!code ++] and [!code --] diff markers in plaintext (txt) and markdown (md) code blocks, which currently do not work because these languages lack comment syntax. Decorations such as: foo [!code --] bar [!code ++] do not work today because the existing decoration logic only handles languages with comment syntax. Plaintext blocks cannot express comments and therefore the current transformer never triggers. Implementation A new transformer is added: packages/core/src/highlight/transformer-plaintext-diff.ts which hooks into the line() transformer stage and applies decorations only when: The language is txt or md The line ends with [!code ++] or [!code --] Finally, the transformer is appended to the built-in transformer chain so it works out-of-the-box. Testing Manually verified with: foo [!code --] bar [!code ++] using: pnpm -F @shikijs/cli exec shiki /absolute/path/example.txt --format html Result is: foo bar No breaking changes Existing behavior for other languages is untouched Decorations continue to work the same for comment-capable languages The new behavior only applies when lang === 'txt' || lang === 'md' Here is attached ss for reference #962

prs.title: fix: enforce ascii_only in terser to ensure ASCII-only minified bundle  fixes(#4043)
prs.body:
This PR fixes #4043.

The minified D3 bundle (`d3.min.js`) previously included non-ASCII characters
such as `µ` and `−`. When these characters appear in a minified JavaScript
bundle, browser engines must represent the entire file as a 2-byte UTF-16
string rather than a 1-byte Latin-1 string, which increases memory usage and
slows down parsing.

This PR updates the Rollup Terser configuration so that the minified output is
ASCII-only.

What was changed
Terser defaults to `ascii_only: false`. This allows Unicode characters to pass
through during minification. To enforce ASCII-only output:

- `format.ascii_only: true` has been added to the Terser configuration.
- The previous use of the `output` key has been merged into the `format` block,
  because Terser requires **either** `format` **or** `output` (not both).
- The preamble banner is now emitted via `format.preamble`.

Updated `terser()` configuration:

```js
terser({
  format: {
    preamble: config.output.banner,
    ascii_only: true
  },
  mangle: {
    reserved: ["InternMap", "InternSet"]
  }
})

prs.title: docs(window): add userland example for detecting drag-stopped and res…
prs.body:
docs(window): add userland example for detecting drag-stopped and resize-stopped events

This PR adds a userland JavaScript example demonstrating how developers can
detect when a window drag or resize operation has *stopped* by listening to
the continuously emitted `tauri://move` and `tauri://resize` events and
debouncing them.

Since native drag-end / resize-end events are not currently exposed by Tauri
and JS event detection is not reliable enough to be implemented in the
runtime, this example provides a recommended, practical solution for
developers needing this behavior.

The new section is added to `learn/window-customization.mdx` where other
window interaction examples (dragging, maximizing, custom titlebars) are
already documented.

prs.title: Add tibetan (bo) language Support
prs.body: This PR adds full Tibetan (bo) language support to DrawDB, providing complete and natural translation for Tibetan-speaking users. All strings have been translated following DrawDB's i18n structure and match the completeness of other supported languages. What’s Included Added bo.js Added Tibetan entry in i18n.js Translation Quality All placeholders preserved ({{tableName}}, {{fieldName}}, etc.) Structure matches English file Consistent terminology across tables, fields, relationships, notes, tasks, and dialogs Testing Verified UI renders correctly in Tibetan Checked dialogs, menus, and forms Build runs without errors e-4456-a611-2d5ce1b87db8" />

prs.title: feat:add Burmese /myanmar (my) language support
prs.body: This PR adds full Burmese (my) language support to DrawDB, delivering a complete and natural translation for Burmese-speaking users. All strings have been translated following DrawDB’s established i18n structure and match the completeness of other supported languages. What’s Included 1. New Language Support Added my.js Added Burmese language entry in i18n.js 2. Translation Quality & Structure All interpolation placeholders preserved e.g., {{tableName}}, {{fieldName}}, {{typeName}}, {{refName}}, etc. File structure exactly mirrors the English source Consistent terminology across: Core menus & actions Tables, fields, relationships Notes, subject areas Validation messages Export/import views 3. UI Integration Burmese appears correctly in Language Selector Switching to Burmese loads all strings properly Testing Verified that all keys and placeholders match the English structure Checked that UI elements render cleanly in Burmese Build runs successfully without warnings Confirmed diagram editing/navigation functionality works normally Screenshot -

prs.title: feat(forms) : allow flexible min/max types in FormValueControl fixes #65676

labels.name: action: review
labels.description: The PR is still awaiting reviews from at least one requested reviewer

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Milestone for triaged issues that have not yet been scheduled for resolution.

prs.body:
feat(forms): allow flexible min and max types in FormUiControl

expand the min and max input types from number-only to string or number
to match native HTML behavior for date and time fields. this change
removes type assignment errors when building custom signal-based form
controls and aligns form inputs with the HTML specification. the update
improves compatibility for developers implementing ControlValueAccessor
or Field bindings that require non numeric min and max values.

issue - #65676

prs.title: feat(window): add drag-stopped and resize-stopped events using move/r…
prs.body:
feat(window-events): add drag-stopped and resize-stopped events

- Added listener for `tauri://move` to detect when window dragging stops
  using a 150ms idle timeout.
- Added listener for `tauri://resize` to detect resize-stop events.
- Ensures consistent behavior across macOS, Windows, and Linux.
This closes #14489

prs.title: Fixed Incorrect parsing of timestamp passing as string
prs.body:
fix(parse): correctly parse numeric string timestamps as timestamps

Previously, dayjs("1762232987879") would attempt to parse the string as a date format, resulting in an incorrect negative timestam and wrong date formatting. This occurred because the regex for date parsing matched the string before checking if it was a numeric timestamp.

Now, numeric string timestamps are checked and parsed as timestamps before attempting date string parsing.

This fix resolves issues with isBefore, isAfter, and formatting methods when using numeric string inputs.

prs.title: Edited dashboard.jsx to Implement real time data synchronization.
prs.body:
Updated the Dashboard component (client/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx) to poll the backend API endpoint /api/activity/active using axios.
The dashboard now fetches the latest active session data periodically and updates the UI with live activity metrics such as start time, end time, and duration.
The browser extension already sends activity data frequently to the backend, enabling near real-time tracking.
